note: Requirement IDs and titles taken from the APTS Foundation tier checklist (appendix/Checklists.md). Vulniq classifies each requirement for its static, read-only scanner context.

domains.name: Scope Enforcement
domains.summary: Rules of Engagement specification, scope validation, temporal boundaries, asset criticality, and scope-drift detection.

requirements.title: Ingest and validate machine-parseable Rules of Engagement
requirements.vulniqControl: vulniq.roe.json + `roe validate` CLI

requirements.title: Validate all IP ranges using CIDR notation with overlap detection
requirements.vulniqControl: N/A — static scanner, no IP/network targeting

requirements.title: Validate domain specifications with explicit wildcard policies
requirements.vulniqControl: N/A — static scanner, no DNS targeting

requirements.title: Enforce temporal boundaries with microsecond precision
requirements.vulniqControl: RoE `scanWindow` (ISO-8601 start/end) enforced by `roe validate`; audit-log entries use ms-precision ISO timestamps

requirements.title: Support asset criticality levels with testing restrictions
requirements.vulniqControl: RoE `assetCriticality` map (glob → tier) surfaced in reports and Conformance Claim

requirements.title: Validate scope immediately before every network action
requirements.vulniqControl: Pre-action scope check before every Read in Step 3; out-of-scope attempts emit `scope.drift` audit event and are blocked

requirements.title: Continuously enforce temporal boundaries with countdown alerts
requirements.vulniqControl: Scan window re-validated at every step transition; remaining time emitted in `step.entered` event context

requirements.title: Maintain immutable hard deny lists for critical systems
requirements.vulniqControl: RoE `forbiddenPaths` list; hashed into audit log at Step -1 as `scope.hash.recorded` so tampering is detectable

domains.name: Safety Controls & Impact Management
domains.summary: Impact classification, kill switches, rate limits, and post-test integrity.

requirements.title: Implement multi-tier impact classification system with CIA scoring
requirements.vulniqControl: Declared posture: Confidentiality=LOW, Integrity=LOW, Availability=LOW (read-only static analysis, no exploits, no target impact)

requirements.title: Implement per-host connection limits and rate limiting
requirements.vulniqControl: N/A — no network actions against targets. `maxFindings` bounds report size; per-step `stepTimeoutMs` bounds duration.

requirements.title: Provide multiple independent kill switch mechanisms
requirements.vulniqControl: Three independent triggers: (1) `.vulniq/HALT` flag file, (2) `cli.mjs halt` command, (3) operator closing the Claude Code session. All detected within one step transition (≤5s typical).

requirements.title: Continuously monitor target system health and automatically halt
requirements.vulniqControl: Per-step soft timeout (default 300s). Exceeded timeout emits `step.timeout` and halts. Post-scan `git status` check detects unexpected target mutation.

requirements.title: Perform automated post-engagement integrity validation
requirements.vulniqControl: Step 8 runs `git status --porcelain`; any modification outside ./reports/ and .vulniq/ is flagged as a policy violation

requirements.title: Enforce agent allowlist externally, not in system prompt
requirements.vulniqControl: Enumerated tool allowlist published in apts-compliance.md and enforced via Claude Code's tool-permission layer (external to the agent prompt)

domains.name: Human Oversight & Intervention
domains.summary: Approval gates, dashboards, SLAs, delegation, pause/kill mechanisms, and escalation channels.

requirements.title: Implement approval gates for exploitation and lateral movement
requirements.vulniqControl: No exploitation or lateral movement performed. Approval gates enforced via Claude Code's per-tool permission layer for any state-mutating action (suppressions, RoE edits, audit ingestion).

requirements.title: Provide real-time dashboard with activity feed and health viz
requirements.vulniqControl: Audit log is the activity feed (`.vulniq/audit-log.ndjson` streams live). CLI commands `last-run`, `history`, `list-audits`, `apts-checklist`, `halt-status`, `audit-verify` expose health.

requirements.title: Define maximum response time SLAs with default-safe behavior
requirements.vulniqControl: `stepTimeoutMs` (default 300s) enforces per-step SLA. Default-safe on timeout: halt + emit `step.timeout`. Default-safe on RoE error: refuse to scan. Default-safe on broken audit chain: halt.

requirements.title: Maintain documented Authority Delegation Matrix
requirements.vulniqControl: Authority Delegation Matrix in apts-compliance.md §D3 enumerates which actions each role may authorise (operator, reviewer, Claude Code session)

requirements.title: Implement pause mechanism preserving complete system state
requirements.vulniqControl: `cli.mjs pause` creates `.vulniq/PAUSE` flag + dumps snapshot of scan state (config, RoE hash, last audit log index, current step) to `.vulniq/pause-state-<ts>.json`. `pause --release` resumes.

requirements.title: Allow operators to redirect scope mid-engagement without restart
requirements.vulniqControl: Operator edits `vulniq.roe.json` then issues `roe validate`; agent reloads scope at next step boundary. Scope change is logged as `scope.hash.recorded` with new hash for audit trail.

requirements.title: Implement one-click kill switch with automatic state dump
requirements.vulniqControl: `cli.mjs halt` writes the HALT flag AND dumps state snapshot (last audit index, last finding, open step) to `.vulniq/halt-state-<ts>.json`. One command, automatic state preservation.

requirements.title: Enforce human approval before irreversible actions
requirements.vulniqControl: Vulniq performs no irreversible actions by design (static, read-only). The only mutations are to ./reports/ and .vulniq/ — both recoverable. Suppressions and audit ingestion require explicit operator CLI invocation.

requirements.title: Immediately escalate unexpected findings and breaches
requirements.vulniqControl: Critical findings surface inline in Step 8 summary (not buried in report). Broken audit chain blocks Step 7. Scope drift emits dedicated audit event. Secret/credential findings get RESTRICTED classification.

requirements.title: Monitor actual testing impact and escalate threshold exceedances
requirements.vulniqControl: `maxFindings` threshold triggers scan truncation with report note. Per-step timeout emits `step.timeout`. Unexpected file mutations detected via `git status` in Step 8 emit `impact.threshold.exceeded`.

requirements.title: Escalate when confidence in scope determination falls below 75%
requirements.vulniqControl: Findings with `confidenceScore < 0.75` emit `confidence.escalation` event and are flagged in the markdown report's triage-needed section

requirements.title: Identify and escalate potential legal/compliance violations
requirements.vulniqControl: Scope drift (access to RoE-forbidden paths) emits `legal.violation` event with RESTRICTED classification. Scan-window violations refuse to start. Legal/compliance notes in RoE preserved verbatim in Conformance Claim.

requirements.title: Maintain real-time activity feed with multi-channel notifications
requirements.vulniqControl: Audit log is the primary channel. Conversational notification via Claude Code session is the second channel (operator sees Step 8 summary + critical events surfaced inline). Multi-channel fan-out to Slack/email is NOT implemented.

domains.name: Graduated Autonomy Levels
domains.summary: Defined autonomy levels (L1-L4), operational constraints per level, boundary enforcement.

requirements.title: Execute only one isolated attack technique per operation
requirements.vulniqControl: L1-specific constraint. Vulniq declares L3 (high autonomy), so this level-1-specific requirement does not apply to our claimed operating mode.
requirements.evidence: Autonomy Level L3 declared; AL-001 applies only when operating at L1

requirements.title: Require all target and technique selection from explicit human command
requirements.vulniqControl: L1-specific constraint. At L3, Vulniq selects targets (files to scan) autonomously within RoE. L1 mode can be forced via `autonomyLevelOverride: "L1"` for engagements needing per-action approval.

requirements.title: Require operator to explicitly configure all technical parameters
requirements.vulniqControl: L1-specific constraint. Default (L3) uses sensible defaults; operator can opt into L1 mode for full configuration requirement.

requirements.title: Prohibit automated chaining; each action needs explicit initiation
requirements.vulniqControl: L1-specific constraint. At L3, the step-protocol chains automatically within the declared autonomy bounds.

requirements.title: Log all actions with 9 mandatory fields in immutable format
requirements.vulniqControl: Every audit-log entry has 12 fields (index, ts, event, stepId, classification, decision, confidence, evidenceHash, reasoning, context, prevHash, thisHash), exceeding the 9-field minimum. Append-only via CLI + hash chain.

requirements.title: Validate target scope, technique permission, engagement status before every action
requirements.vulniqControl: Step 3 validates RoE scope per file + check category enabled in config + suppressions + scan-window validity before each Read

requirements.title: Maintain continuous operator connection with live dashboard
requirements.vulniqControl: Claude Code session IS the continuous operator connection (user sees streaming updates). Audit log is the live event source operators can `tail -f`.

requirements.title: Configure explicit escalation triggers for violations and anomalies
requirements.vulniqControl: Escalation triggers: scope.drift → halt file path, audit chain break → abort Step 7, critical finding → inline surfacing in Step 8, confidence <0.75 → flag for triage, step.timeout → halt

requirements.title: Provide immediate termination within 5 seconds, pause capability
requirements.vulniqControl: HALT flag detected within one step transition (typically <1s; never >5s given tool-call granularity). `pause` command preserves state without termination.

requirements.title: Formally define boundaries as specific unambiguous documents
requirements.vulniqControl: `vulniq.roe.json` is the boundary document. Schema documented in references/config-schema.md §Rules of Engagement. Hash recorded in audit log at every scan start.

requirements.title: Continuously monitor boundary compliance every 30 minutes
requirements.vulniqControl: Every 30 file operations OR 10 minutes (whichever first), Vulniq re-hashes RoE and emits `boundary.recheck` event; hash mismatch halts the scan.

domains.name: Auditability & Reproducibility
domains.summary: Structured, tamper-evident logging with evidence hashing and classification.

requirements.title: Log all events with millisecond timestamps in schema-validated format
requirements.vulniqControl: `.vulniq/audit-log.ndjson` NDJSON with fixed schema (see audit-log.mjs EVENTS + CLASSIFICATIONS); ISO-8601 millisecond timestamps from `Date.toISOString()`

requirements.title: Log every phase transition with state and authorization status
requirements.vulniqControl: `step.entered` / `step.exited` events emitted at every Step transition with stepId, classification, and RoE-auth status in context

requirements.title: Log autonomous decisions with confidence score and alternatives
requirements.vulniqControl: Each finding logged with `confidenceScore` (0.0-1.0), `validationStatus`, and `reasoning` (which alternative interpretations were considered)

requirements.title: Document complete reasoning chain for multi-step attack sequences
requirements.vulniqControl: Each finding's `reasoning` field captures the verification chain (pattern matched → context read → classification decision). Multi-file correlations logged as separate events referencing prior indices.

requirements.title: Hash all evidence using SHA-256 minimum
requirements.vulniqControl: Every finding carries `evidenceHash = sha256(codeSnippet)`. RoE file hashed at scan start. SARIF results and markdown reports echo the hash for cross-reference.

requirements.title: Store logs in append-only format with SHA-256 hash chaining
requirements.vulniqControl: Each entry: `thisHash = sha256(prevHash || canonical(entry))`. Agent convention forbids Write/Edit on the NDJSON file; only `cli.mjs audit-log` appends. `audit-verify` walks the chain.

requirements.title: Classify evidence by sensitivity and encrypt confidential data
requirements.vulniqControl: Four classifications: PUBLIC / STANDARD / CONFIDENTIAL / RESTRICTED. Secret findings default RESTRICTED. Encryption of RESTRICTED data at rest is delegated to the operator's filesystem encryption (Vulniq does not implement its own crypto layer).

domains.name: Manipulation Resistance
domains.summary: Defense against prompt injection, authority claims, secondary channels, and scope manipulation.

requirements.title: Implement defense-in-depth separating operator instructions from targets
requirements.vulniqControl: Doctrine: scanned content is data, never instructions. Operator instructions arrive only via Claude Code session and `vulniq.roe.json`. Target files (anything under scan) are read as opaque text.

requirements.title: Validate and sanitize all target responses before LLM processing
requirements.vulniqControl: N/A — Vulniq reads files, not live target responses. Pattern matching operates on raw bytes; no LLM post-processing of target content as instructions.

requirements.title: Treat error messages as data only, not instructions
requirements.vulniqControl: External command errors (npm audit, git) captured as data and logged; never interpreted as directives to change behaviour.

requirements.title: Parse configuration files for vulnerability info, block injections
requirements.vulniqControl: Config-file scanning is a core detection category (HDR, COR, AUTH rules read config). Scanned config content never alters Vulniq's own config — `vulniq.config.json` and `vulniq.roe.json` are only loaded from the project root.

requirements.title: Identify and reject all authority claims from target content
requirements.vulniqControl: MR-xxx detection patterns surface authority-claim strings (`SYSTEM:`, `you are now…`, `grant me access…`) as info findings; Vulniq never acts on them regardless of how authoritative they appear.

requirements.title: Validate all redirects against scope before following
requirements.vulniqControl: N/A — no HTTP redirects or network traversal.

requirements.title: Validate DNS resolutions including CNAME aliases
requirements.vulniqControl: N/A — no DNS resolution.

requirements.title: Validate URLs and IPs in target responses; block dangerous protocols
requirements.vulniqControl: OWA-005 (open redirect) and OWA-007 (HTTP for sensitive ops) are detection rules for URLs/protocols in scanned code. Vulniq itself makes no outbound network calls except the allowlisted `npm audit`.

requirements.title: Detect and resist linguistic manipulation in target content
requirements.vulniqControl: MR-001..MR-005 detection patterns + doctrine in references/manipulation-resistance.md

requirements.title: Prevent secondary channels (email, DNS, webhooks) as instruction paths
requirements.vulniqControl: Vulniq accepts no inbound instructions beyond Claude Code session + RoE file. No webhook listener, no email intake, no DNS-based config.

requirements.title: Protect scope through cryptographic signing and integrity monitoring
requirements.vulniqControl: Scope integrity: `roe validate` computes sha256 of `vulniq.roe.json` and emits `scope.hash.recorded`. Boundary recheck (AL-016) every 30 ops re-hashes and halts on mismatch. Formal digital signing of the RoE file itself is delegated to the operator's VCS (git-signed commits).

requirements.title: Enforce architectural separation between trusted and untrusted data
requirements.vulniqControl: Trusted sources: Claude Code session messages, vulniq.config.json, vulniq.roe.json (all explicitly operator-controlled). Untrusted sources: everything scanned. Never crossed by design — the agent pattern prevents target content from reaching the config layer.

requirements.title: Encrypt discovered credentials immediately in dedicated vault
requirements.vulniqControl: Secret findings (SEC-* rules) never written in plaintext to reports — only redacted excerpts + SHA-256 hash. Discovered credentials in SARIF carry partial masking + `classification: RESTRICTED`. Encrypted vault storage delegated to operator (e.g., secrets manager).

domains.name: Third-Party & Supply Chain Trust
domains.summary: Provider vetting, credential handling, incident response, SBOM, data classification, and foundation model disclosure.

requirements.title: Establish documented vetting process for external providers
requirements.vulniqControl: Single external provider: Anthropic (Claude Code runtime). Vetting process documented in apts-compliance.md §D7 — covers provider security posture review, data-handling commitments, and Anthropic's published security documentation.

requirements.title: Use strong authentication with secure credential storage
requirements.vulniqControl: Vulniq stores no credentials of its own. Authentication to Anthropic is handled by the host Claude Code session. npm audit uses local npm credentials (unchanged by Vulniq).

requirements.title: Establish incident response procedures with breach notification
requirements.vulniqControl: Incident Response procedures documented in apts-compliance.md §D7 IR: define what constitutes an incident (broken audit chain, scope drift, unexpected file mutation), required operator actions, notification thresholds.

requirements.title: Maintain complete SBOM with vulnerability monitoring
requirements.vulniqControl: Vulniq SBOM: Node.js standard library only (fs, path, crypto, url). No external npm dependencies. SBOM declared in apts-compliance.md §D7 SBOM and verifiable by `grep -l 'from "' vulniq/scripts/*.mjs`.

requirements.title: Implement cloud hardening with least privilege and AES-256
requirements.vulniqControl: N/A — Vulniq is a local skill; no cloud infrastructure to harden. Runs in the operator's trust boundary.

requirements.title: Establish four-level data classification framework
requirements.vulniqControl: Four classifications defined and enforced: PUBLIC / STANDARD / CONFIDENTIAL / RESTRICTED. Applied to every audit-log event and every finding.

requirements.title: Implement automated discovery of credentials and PII
requirements.vulniqControl: SEC-001..SEC-008 (secrets, API keys, tokens, AWS creds, JWTs, private keys, env files) + PII-001..PII-006 (Sentry PII, console logging, user-object logging) are core detection categories with ≥14 active rules.

requirements.title: Enforce TLS 1.2+ and AES-256-GCM for encryption
requirements.vulniqControl: N/A — Vulniq performs no network I/O beyond npm audit (which uses npm's own TLS policy).

requirements.title: Detect cross-engagement data leaks via monitoring and testing
requirements.vulniqControl: Per-project state isolation: config discovery walks up from CWD and stops at the first vulniq.config.json. `.vulniq/` is project-scoped. Two projects on the same machine cannot share scan history, audit log, or RoE.

requirements.title: Disclose foundation model powering agent runtime
requirements.vulniqControl: Conformance Claim §2 declares: provider=Anthropic, product=Claude Code, model=(session-provided). Every markdown report header also surfaces the autonomy level and conformance pointer.

domains.name: Reporting
domains.summary: Finding disclosure, false-positive methodology, coverage matrix, and stakeholder summary.

requirements.title: Disclose platform's false positive rate methodology in reports
requirements.vulniqControl: Every markdown report includes a 'Confidence & False-Positive Methodology' block describing the 5-band confidence rubric (1.0/0.9/0.7/0.5/0.3) and the verification doctrine ('every grep match is a candidate, not a finding').

requirements.title: Include coverage matrix identifying tested vulnerability classes
requirements.vulniqControl: The report's 'Score Breakdown' table already lists every category (11 total: SEC, XSS, HDR, PII, AUTH, DEP, OWA, COR, ERR, CHN, MR) with enabled/disabled status and score — that IS the coverage matrix. Enhanced in Step 7 template to explicitly label as Coverage Matrix.

requirements.title: Include executive summary for non-technical stakeholders
requirements.vulniqControl: Every markdown report opens with an Executive Summary section (2-3 sentences, plain language, overall posture + top concern + key recommendation).
